My presentation is, to introduce people to me and that handoff is all I'm asking for is a networking connection thing, right? I'll tell you about a book I read that kind of gave me this perspective on business. It's called The Go-Giver.

If you want to receive value from the world, the best way to earn it is by giving it away first. So one way to look at that in business is when I meet a business owner, like let's say they're I say, well, what's one problem you're having today operationally? And I say, okay, well, I can help you solve that. And then I tell them, mainly, I'd like to put my point of sale system in your restaurant. But ideally, I'm helping you solve a problem to earn your trust.

So what's that look like? Just last week, I've been talking to this lady about a restaurant for six months. She kept telling me, yeah, I'm interested, but she never committed to me, right? She won't sign the paperwork. So I finally said, lady, here's the document. I want you to sign the deal with me today. You keep telling me you'd like to, but you won't commit to me, right? It's kind of like dating, dating girls with their situationships these days.

So I came to her and I said, "Here's the deal. "I'm a grown man, I'm a businessman, "I value my time, I value your time. I want to help you, but I can't help you if you won't help yourself. What can I do for you today that proves to you that I'm here for the long term? She said, well, my kitchen refrigerator door to my walk-in cooler broke. I need a new one and I don't have time to figure that out.

I found her one within two hours and it delivered within two days. And I saved her, instead of me making money on it, because I have a restaurant vendor, equipment vendor, instead of me taking money on it, to her at a discount and now I'm making $1,200 a month on a restaurant. She knows that. I know that. Everybody's happy. But I solved the problem for her. Anyways, but that problem, I met this lady because this Georgia student, I don't want to tell you his name because he's a UGA player and he just keeps things private, but he went in there after a game, met her, and told her she needed to call me.

She calls me and I meet with her. I met her in person, sat down with her and I gave her some advice and she said thank you. And, you know, she just was like, yeah, I'll take it into consideration. And I was like, okay, well. consideration is let's sign the paperwork. You know, it sounds good, right? So everything sounds good. You know, if it sounds good, why not?
